MINI MINUTES: Case heard with PES-15-298463 In the Matter of the Harrington 1989 Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Denis Chambliss for and with Eriska Harrington. Dennis M. Sullivan for the Walter Harrington Foundation. James M. Wagstaff, Adrian J. Sawyer and Brady Dewar for Paul Harrington, Mark Harrington and Gloria Greco.

Mr. Wagstaff present his oral argument in support of his position regarding his objections the the tenative ruling. Ms. Chambliss present her rebuttal argument in support of her position. Mr. Dewar explained the hand written notation on the court filed document was indeed not signed Eriska but that he was instructed by the Recorders office to write that specifc language. Mr.Sullivan presented his oral arguement in support of his positon regarding the settlement agreement. Regarding the hold amounts, Ms. Chambliss states the following: 1) $150 per day from 5/19/17 to 7/26/17 with the total amount of $20,100.00. 2)$3531.83 as 6/20/17 3) Net is $16,568.17

After hearing oral argument and reviewing the court filed papers, the Court takes the matters under submission.

MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-14-298311 The Estate of Walter J. Harrington & PTR-15-298463 The Harrington 1989 Revocable Trust.
Appearances: Adrian Sawyer & Brady Dewar for John Harrington, Mark Harrington, Paul Harrington & Gloria Greco. J. Timothy Maximoff & James Cummins for Erishka Harrington.

The Court stated that it looks like this is expanding and asked for the status on mediation. Mr. Sawyer stated that he substituted in after the mediation, that he does not think that the parties were close and that they are currently involved in discovery. The Court asked if there would be dispositive motions. Mr. Maximoff stated that discovery is ongoing, that they want to keep on a quick path, that recent filings have expanded the scope and that they could be ready for trial by November. The Court stated that it does not want to set this for trial if the parties are not ready, that it would like to set a Mandatory Settlement Conference (MSC) before Judge Wiss but that if they think it is premature he will not do so. Mr. Sawyer stated that both sides have additional witnesses, that setting trial for November might be ambitious, that they think the case may be ready to be set in November and asked that the MSC be set for mid to late December. The Court stated that it hates to have the parties come back for trial setting and asked if the MSC is set for December would it be a game plan to set trial for April 2017. Mr. Maximoff stated that he cannot speak for Ms. Chambliss. Mr. Sawyer stated that Mr. Wagstaff has a trial set for April. The Court proposed a trial date of May 22, 2017, stated that it will not sign the order today, requested that the parties decide and inform the court as to that date and that it will sign the order referring the parties to Judge Wiss for MSC. Mr. Maximoff stated that they have a pending Heggstad and that they do not want to hold this up further.

Subsequent to the hearing the parties confirmed that they will accept the trial date of May 22, 2017 and an order was signed and sent out.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Will Examination
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This came on calendar for a forensic will examination pursuant to the court's 1-29-16 orders.

In Appearance: Steven P. Braccini, Esq. appearing for Mr. John Harrington, petitioner/moving party Mr. James R. Cummins, Esq. for objector/respondent Eriksha Harrington, Forensic Will Examiner David S. Moore, (916)298-3205 and Deputy Court Clerk Diane Hakewill and Deputy Court Clerk Lydia Paredes.

The will examination was scheduled to begin at 1:30pm in the Room 202 conference room. David Moore arrived at 1:50pm. The will examination began at 1:54 pm. Mr. Moore scanned the original wills onto his laptop.

Mr. Moore proceeded to examine the will on site with the above-named in appearance. He performed the following tasks on the seven page original will: Holding the will up to the light, comparing edges of the document, comparing the original will, including signatures, to other signed paperwork, and comparing each sheet to one another within the will. Throughout the will examination, he took approximately three pages of notes.

The Deputy Court Clerks, Diane Hakewill and Lydia Paredes, were present during the entire will examination. When they left the conference room to make inquiries, they had the original wills in their possession at all times. The will examination ended at 2:30 pm.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Will Contest
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Braccini & Erika Gasaway for John Harrington who is present in court. Jeff Loew for Paul Harrington. Denis Chambliss for (petitioner/moving party) Erishka Harrington who is present in court.

Ms. Chambliss stated that she is here to argue two points, the first is the issue of forgery, that her client satisfied the burden, that it is disputed by declarations of decedent's sons but that their statements are based on belief and not on personal knowledge. The Court stated that personal knowledge based on having been there is not required here. Ms. Chambliss stated that they needed to take the next step such as they know their father's signature and this is not it. The Court stated that the premise of their statement of belief is familiarity with the handwriting, that it is not very strong but that the court does not get to weigh the evidence. Ms. Chambliss stated that they believe the evidence is proper before the court regarding meeting the burden and that if the sons had stronger evidence they should have brought it. The Court stated that they will be able to do so at trial and that objections against such are apt to be overruled. Ms. Chambliss stated that they need personal knowledge which is not present, that in regards to the issue of active participation they argue that it is not part of their case but the will contest pleads active participation, that the motion is directed on active participation and undue burden on the issue of making the case to shift the burden. The Court asked how it can grant summary adjudication on one fact that would not resolve a claim. Ms. Chambliss stated that it is as to the claim of shifting of presumption. The Court stated that it does not resolve the issue of undue influence, asked what authority they have and that summary adjudication requires a disposition of a claim. Ms. Chambliss replied that the authorities are in their motion. The Court stated that active participation is a factor, that the Probate Code says to look to the Welfare & Institutions Code which tells us all factors are to be weighed together. Mr. Braccini stated that they agree with the tentative. The Court stated that it will adopt the tentative ruling for the reasons indicated in the tentative and what has been discussed this afternoon and provided additional reasoning.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Court Reporter: Donielle Del Carlo, CSR #10476, 415-357-4300 (Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Continued To Oct-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-14-298311 Estate of Harrington and PTR-15-298463 The Harrington 1989 Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Sarah Sheppard for (objector) Paul Harrington. Tomasene Knight for (beneficiary) Gloria Greco. John Minton for (beneficiary) Mark Harrington. Steve Braccini & Erika Gassoway for and with (beneficiary) John Harrington. Denis Chambliss for and with Erishka Harrington. Dennis Sullivan for the Walter J. Harrington Foundation.

The Court stated that there are competing petitions for probate, a petition to invalidate 2002 trust documents and asked if the parties have talked about how to resolve the issues. Ms. Chambliss stated that mediation is not appropriate and that they want a date for a prove-up hearing of the 2002 will. Mr. Braccini stated that they would like a 90-100 day continuance to talk and then may need to set an evidentiary hearing. The court stated that it may be with discovery starting it is too early to talk about mediation and that there are a lot of disputed issues here. Ms. Chambliss suggested a 60 day continuance to come back for a status, that they will do an initial round of discovery and meet and confer and that she does not expect any Motions to Compel coming out of that. Mr. Braccini stated that a 60 day return is too soon, that they have 20 percipient witnesses to depose, that he filed pleadings late although nothing new was raised, that he would like to zero in on the issue today if Ms. Harrington should use trust funds to contest and that it is not an attack on the trust as a whole. Mr. Braccini further stated that in regards to the estate matters there has been a delay in appointing a personal representative, that probate was first opened in Santa Clara County, that Ms. Harrington objected and had the death certificate changed to San Francisco, that there are numerous parcels of real estate, that a Heggstad petition has been filed, that they will be objecting to the petition, that a personal representative is necessary to preserve and protect the assets and asks that Boston Private Bank be appointed. The Court stated that the appointment of a special administer is not before the court, that this cannot be taken up today and that the parties should talk to confirm that there is a need and how to fill it. The Court further stated that it should be apparent that this matter will be enormously expensive, that it cannot be in the best interest of the family to spend that much money, that it is hard to imagine what they will gain, that it will not enjoin the trustee from defending the attack, that the trustee has an interest in what is spent and that it may lead the trustee to think about separate counsel or a professional trustee. Mr. Minton asked if the issue of the use of trust funds something that will be taken up at a later date. The Court stated that it does not want to litigate over and over, that there is an internal way of handling the issues, that the cases cited would not compel the court to remove the trustee and urged everyone to think of ways to limit the litigation. The petition for letters of administration with will annexed and the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (case PES-14-298311) and the petition to invalidate trust amendment and to enjoin trustee from using trust funds to defend trust contest (case PTR-15-298463)were continued to October 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
